title: Features schema description drops "Epic-level" qualifier; canonical kind name is "feature"
context: PM-vocabulary survey (analysis/2026-05-05-pm-vocabulary-prior-art-survey.md) catalogues "epic" as Scrum/SAFe-origin emphasis-on-scale qualifier. User direction 2026-05-14: "epic" reads as zero-signal. The features.schema.json description prose carried "L3 Work layer. Epic-level features with..." which conflated PM-jargon scale-emphasis with the canonical block name. The structural shape (feature → stories → tasks + scoped findings) is what carries meaning; the "epic-level" qualifier adds no machine semantics and reads opaque to non-PM-familiar readers.
decision: Drop "Epic-level" from features.schema.json description prose. Canonical kind name is "feature" (singular) / "features" (block array_key). config.block_kinds[].display_name will read "Features" (matching schema.title). No PM-scale qualifier in schema prose or display layer.
consequences: Schema prose reads cleanly to non-PM readers.
Display-name layer can rely on schema.title field directly (see DEC-0023).
Future block-kind authoring avoids PM-jargon qualifiers in descriptions.
No code paths or AJV behavior changes (description field is not enforced).
